mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, which lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy path is still declining.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are negative or below baseline in more than 60% of months when FEDFUNDS has declined for at least 2 consecutive observations, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a recent floor signals weakening household income and demand, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations generate positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer spending, which supports revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when sales growth is broad-based rather than transitory.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 60-day SPX returns occur in less than 55% of periods with a 3-month rising RSAFS trend, or if the relationship vanishes in every rolling 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO drops sharply while CPIAUCSL continues to rise, the economy is experiencing stagflationary supply stress that raises margin pressure and should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls by at least 1.0% over 2 months while CPIAUCSL rises over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if the combined condition does not yield negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of triggered cases, or if either proxy alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts capture interest-rate-sensitive demand; a rebound in HOUST from depressed levels signals improving growth expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through cyclical sentiment channels.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by at least 5% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if breakout months in HOUST fail to deliver above-baseline 5-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate is not at least 5 percentage points above unconditional.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with a falling unemployment rate signals labor-market healing and higher future consumption, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings breadth improves.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 60-day SPX returns are not concentrated in months with rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE, or if the interaction term is not stronger than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A high and rising FEDFUNDS rate tightens discount rates and liquidity conditions, so when policy is above a restrictive threshold it should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not show worse 20-day SPX returns than periods below the threshold, or if the conditional hit rate is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates broad demand expansion, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through stronger sales and earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous quarterly GDP and monthly retail-sales growth does not outperform either proxy alone, or if the next-60-day SPX return is not positive in a majority of synchronized-growth periods.
